title: micromyrtus littoralis
intro: Micromyrtus littoralis, also known as coastal heath myrtle, is a small shrub native to the coastal regions of Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Micromyrtus littoralis include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
